## PAGE 05621: Managing used projects

- page_id: `243966145`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243966145/Managing+used+projects
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-09-12T10:50:21.104+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Working with projects > Project partitioning
- labels: ['used-projects', 'msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can manage usages of an open local project in the**Used Projects**dialog. To learn how to manage Teamwork Cloud project usages, go to[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing project usages' space='CM'].

To specify project usage options

1. Do one of the following: • From the Options menu, select Project Usages . • Right-click a used project in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Containment tab' space=''] and, from its shortcut menu, select Project Usages > Options . The Used Projects dialog opens. [ATTACHMENT filename='used_projects_dialog.png']
2. Do one or both of the following:
  - Specify the following project usage options: Dialog element nameDescription**Accessibility****Read-only**- the project is loaded for reading only and cannot be modified in the main project. Elements of the used project can be used and referenced in the main project.**Read-write**- the project can be edited directly in the main project. Elements of this used project can be used, referenced, and modified in the main project.**Load Mode****Always load** (default) - the selected used project is always loaded when the main project is opened.**Autoload** - the selected used project is not loaded when the main project is opened. The modeling tool monitors user activities in the project and loads the particular used project on demand by the project.**Autoload with prompt** - this mode is similar to the **Autoload** mode, except that the modeling tool asks the user for a confirmation before loading the used project.**Manual load** - the selected used project is not loaded when the main project is opened. The model integrity is not broken, as all required elements of the used project exist as simplified versions (that is, loaded as proxies) of the elements used in the project. This load mode is recommended for all used projects that are stable or rarely modified. 
Reload the main project after changing the load mode of the selected used project.**Use Index**Select this check box to load indexed elements from the used project that is not loaded. This option is available only if the used project is not loaded. Using indexed elements in the project increases the performance.**Packages**this is the list of all shared packages of a used project and paths for mounting these packages in the main project.
  - Use the following commands: CommandDescription**Use Project**Click to select projects for usage in the open project and specify the usage options. The **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using other projects in a project' space='']** wizard opens.**Remove** > **Remove with References**Click to remove the selected used project from the project. After removal, all the elements of the used project disappear from the Containment tree, and all the references to these elements are cleared from other places in the model.**Remove** > **Remove, keep references**Click to remove the selected used project and keep model references across it in the model. There are two cases when you may need to remove the used project this way:**Case #1**: You may need to replace a used project with the equivalent project carrying the same elements (but with slightly different content) and retaining all the references.In this case, removing the initially used project, but keeping references creates an automated project usage that temporarily carries the model level references. This automated usage is invalid, since it is unconfirmed. You can confirm it by [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resolving unconfirmed usages of packages' space=''].**Case #2**: You may need to refactor projects that are created with the modeling tool versions earlier than 17.0.3 and remove excessive project usages. These versions of the modeling tool have only user-defined project usages. Because of this, a project usually had direct user defined usages to indirectly used projects besides the indirect usages through directly used projects. So both “project → directly used project → indirectly used project” and “project → indirectly used project” usage paths were present. In this case it may be advantageous to remove the surplus usage “project → indirectly used project” by clicking the Remove, keep References button.**Import**Click to import the contents of the selected used project to the main project. The Import button is available if the selected used project:Is not a MagicDraw native projectIs locked (valid for server projects only)Is not locked by another user (valid for server projects only)The same used project can be imported only once. After the import, all elements of the used project are copied to the main project. The inner structure of the used project is included in the project as a package. The imported project is not deleted from the file system.Standard and system profiles cannot be imported.Used projects containing recovered elements cannot be imported. If you attempt to do so, an informational error message is displayed, halting the import. In this case, review and resolve the recovered element validation errors in the Validation Results panel. For more information, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing unresolved references' space=''].**Reload**Click to reload the selected used project in the main project. This button is available only when the selected used project is loaded.**Unload**Click to unload the selected used project from the main project leaving only used elements loaded. It is recommended to unload rarely used projects in order to reduce memory usage while working with large projects.This button is available when a selected used project is loaded.**Open Project**Click to open the selected used project for editing.**Options**Click to open the **Project Options** dialog to manage styles and element display settings of the selected used project. It allows users to change element styles in the used project even without opening it.
3. Click**OK**.

**Related pages**

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">You can manage usages of an open local project in the </span><strong>Used Projects</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> dialog. To learn how to manage Teamwork Cloud project usages, go to </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="CM" ri:content-title="Managing project usages" /></ac:link><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">.</span></p><p>To specify project usage options</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="5dc3f5b1-b8b2-411f-9fa2-7a02e1a0b813">Do one of the following:<br />• From the <strong>Options</strong> menu, select <strong>Project Usages</strong>.<br />• Right-click a used project in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Containment tab" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Containment tree]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and, from its shortcut menu, select <strong>Project Usages</strong> &gt; <strong>Options</strong>.<br />The <strong>Used Projects</strong> dialog opens.<br /><br /><ac:image ac:title="Used Projects dialog" ac:alt="Used Projects dialog"><ri:attachment ri:filename="used_projects_dialog.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li data-uuid="45275fad-2c44-45ed-a86f-cea11139341a"><p>Do one or both of the following:</p><ul><li><p>Specify the following project usage options:</p><table><colgroup class=""><col class="" /><col class="" /></colgroup><tbody class=""><tr class=""><th>Dialog element name</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr class=""><td><strong>Accessibility</strong></td><td><ul><li><strong>Read-only </strong>- the project is loaded for reading only and cannot be modified in the main project. Elements of the used project can be used and referenced in the main project.</li><li><strong>Read-write </strong>- the project can be edited directly in the main project. Elements of this used project can be used, referenced, and modified in the main project.</li></ul></td></tr><tr class=""><td><strong>Load Mode</strong></td><td><ul><li><p><strong>Always load</strong> (default) - the selected used project is always loaded when the main project is opened.</p></li><li><p><strong>Autoload</strong> - the selected used project is not loaded when the main project is opened. The modeling tool monitors user activities in the project and loads the particular used project on demand by the project.</p></li><li><p><strong>Autoload with prompt</strong> - this mode is similar to the <strong>Autoload</strong> mode, except that the modeling tool asks the user for a confirmation before loading the used project.</p></li><li><p><strong>Manual load</strong> - the selected used project is not loaded when the main project is opened. The model integrity is not broken, as all required elements of the used project exist as simplified versions (that is, loaded as proxies) of the elements used in the project. This load mode is recommended for all used projects that are stable or rarely modified.<br />Reload the main project after changing the load mode of the selected used project.</p></li></ul></td></tr><tr class=""><td><strong>Use Index</strong></td><td>Select this check box to load indexed elements from the used project that is not loaded. This option is available only if the used project is not loaded. Using indexed elements in the project increases the performance.</td></tr><tr class=""><td><strong>Packages</strong></td><td>this is the list of all shared packages of a used project and paths for mounting these packages in the main project.</td></tr></tbody></table></li></ul><p><span> </span></p><ul><li><p>Use the following commands:</p><table><colgroup class=""><col class="" /><col class="" /></colgroup><tbody class=""><tr class=""><th>Command</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr class=""><td><strong>Use Project</strong></td><td><p>Click to select projects for usage in the open project and specify the usage options. The <strong> <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Using other projects in a project" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Use Project]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> </strong> wizard opens.</p></td></tr><tr class=""><td><strong>Remove</strong> &gt; <strong>Remove with References</strong></td><td><p>Click to remove the selected used project from the project. After removal, all the elements of the used project disappear from the Containment tree, and all the references to these elements are cleared from other places in the model.</p></td></tr><tr class=""><td><strong>Remove</strong> &gt; <strong>Remove, keep references</strong></td><td><p>Click to remove the selected used project and keep model references across it in the model. There are two cases when you may need to remove the used project this way:</p><p><strong>Case #1</strong>: You may need to replace a used project with the equivalent project carrying the same elements (but with slightly different content) and retaining all the references.</p><p>In this case, removing the initially used project, but keeping references creates an automated project usage that temporarily carries the model level references. This automated usage is invalid, since it is unconfirmed. You can confirm it by <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Resolving unconfirmed usages of packages" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[replacing the initially used project with another project]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p><p><strong>Case #2</strong>: You may need to refactor projects that are created with the modeling tool versions earlier than 17.0.3 and remove excessive project usages. These versions of the modeling tool have only user-defined project usages. Because of this, a project usually had direct user defined usages to indirectly used projects besides the indirect usages through directly used projects. So both “project → directly used project → indirectly used project” and “project → indirectly used project” usage paths were present. In this case it may be advantageous to remove the surplus usage “project → indirectly used project” by clicking the Remove, keep References button.</p></td></tr><tr class=""><td><strong>Import</strong></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Click to import the contents of the selected used project to the main project. The Import button is available if the selected used project:</p><ul><li>Is not a MagicDraw native project</li><li>Is locked (valid for server projects only)</li><li>Is not locked by another user (valid for server projects only)</li></ul><p>The same used project can be imported only once. After the import, all elements of the used project are copied to the main project. The inner structure of the used project is included in the project as a package. The imported project is not deleted from the file system.</p><p>Standard and system profiles cannot be imported.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c0e458b7-430e-4a88-86c5-24f894158870"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Used projects containing recovered elements cannot be imported. If you attempt to do so, an informational error message is displayed, halting the import. In this case, review and resolve the recovered element validation errors in the Validation Results panel. For more information, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Managing unresolved references" /></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr class=""><td><strong>Reload</strong></td><td><p>Click to reload the selected used project in the main project. This button is available only when the selected used project is loaded.</p></td></tr><tr class=""><td><strong>Unload</strong></td><td><p>Click to unload the selected used project from the main project leaving only used elements loaded. It is recommended to unload rarely used projects in order to reduce memory usage while working with large projects.</p><p>This button is available when a selected used project is loaded.</p></td></tr><tr class=""><td><strong>Open Project</strong></td><td>Click to open the selected used project for editing.</td></tr><tr class=""><td><strong>Options</strong></td><td><p>Click to open the <strong>Project Options</strong> dialog to manage styles and element display settings of the selected used project. It allows users to change element styles in the used project even without opening it.</p></td></tr></tbody></table></li></ul></li><li data-uuid="b6ee2f07-b356-4461-a196-33c4405446f3"><p><span>Click </span><span> </span><strong>OK</strong><span>.</span></p></li></ol><p><br /></p><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="f4647c59-65ba-4a55-ae28-170f4669199b" /></p>
````

## PAGE 05623: Manipulations in the relation map

- page_id: `243966604`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243966604/Manipulations+in+the+relation+map
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-07T17:22:38.087+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Diagrams > Analysis diagrams > Relation Map
- labels: ['relation-map-suppress-expand', 'relation-map-zoom', 'relation-map-hide', 'relation-map-remove', 'relation-map-restore-refresh', 'relation-map-validate', 'relation-map-decompose', 'relation-map-create-element', 'msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can manipulate and analyze your relation map in the following ways:

- / branches
- Move /
- / / /
- / an element
- 
- 
- 
- 
- 
- 

suppressTo suppress branches

- Click [ATTACHMENT filename='suppress_button.png'] near the Node.

expandTo expand branches

- Click [ATTACHMENT filename='expand_button.png'] near the Node.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

move_structureTo move the relation map structure

- Click on an empty place in the Relation Map and drag it. The whole structure will be moved appropriately.

move_nodeTo move the selected Node

- Click a Node and drag it. The selected Node will be moved appropriately.

zoom_inTo zoom in the relation map structure

Do one of the following:

- Hold down Ctrl while scrolling up.
- Click the Zoom In button [ATTACHMENT filename='zoom_in.png'] on the toolbar.
- Press Ctrl+NumPad +.

zoom_outTo zoom out the relation map structure

Do one of the following:

- Hold down Ctrl while scrolling down.
- Click the Zoom Out button [ATTACHMENT filename='zoom_out.png'] on the toolbar.
- Press Ctrl+NumPad -.

fitTo fit in window

Do one of the following:

- Click the Fit in Window button [ATTACHMENT filename='fit_in_window.png'] on the toolbar.
- Press Ctrl+W.

zoom_1_1To zoom 1:1

- Click the Zoom 1:1 button [ATTACHMENT filename='zoom_1_1.png'] on the toolbar.
- Press Ctrl+NumPad /.

hideTo hide an existing element in the diagram

- Press Delete.

removeTo remove an existing element from the model

- Press Ctrl+D.

restoreTo restore the layout of the manually suppressed/expanded branches, moved, removed and hidden symbols

- Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the View toolbar. If the**Preserve manually suppressed/expanded branches and hidden elements** option in the **Environment Options** dialog > **Diagrams** > **Relation Map**, is selected, the layout of moved elements is restored.

invalidTo start the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Validation' space='']of the relation map

- Click the Validate Diagram button [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the toolbar of that diagram, table, matrix, or map. The diagram is validated against all validation rules stored in the model, except those that are ignored.

containment_treeTo select an element in the Containment tree

- Select the element and click [ATTACHMENT filename='select_in_containment_tree.png'] on the Navigation toolbar.
- On the selected element shortcut menu, click Select in Containment Tree .

decomposeTo decompose an element

1. Select an element.
2. Do one of the following:
  - From the selected element shortcut menu, select Create Related Element .
  - Click [ATTACHMENT filename='new_element_button.png'] or press Insert (or [ATTACHMENT filename='command-button.jpg'] + I).
3. Select element type and relationship. If selected element type exists in the model, select an appropriate element from the list to represent it in the relation map. >]]> 
The element is decomposed.

openTo open an existing element list for the unnamed element

1. Select unnamed element.
2. Do one of the following: - Click the left mouse button. - Press Space. An existing element list opens, if such elements already exist in the model.

same_typeTo create element of the same type

1. Type element name.
2. Press Ctrl+Enter while in the name edit mode.

restored.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ul><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="41d38c8a-ccdb-4a9c-a4ce-e771f051e101"><ac:parameter ac:name="">invalid</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To start the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Validation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[validation ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>of the relation map</p><hr /><ul><li>Click the Validate Diagram button <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:title="Validate Diagram" ac:alt="Validate Diagram"><ri:attachment ri:filename="validate_diagram.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span> on the toolbar of that diagram, table, matrix, or map. The diagram is validated against all validation rules stored in the model, except those that are ignored.</li></ul><p> </p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="db975f0e-96b7-4a2f-bc7c-194149d5cec7"><ac:parameter ac:name="">containment_tree</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To select an element in the Containment tree</p><hr /><ul><li>Select the element and click <ac:image ac:title="Select an element in the Containment tree" ac:alt="Select an element in the Containment tree"><ri:attachment ri:filename="select_in_containment_tree.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> on the Navigation toolbar.</li><li>On the selected element shortcut menu, click<strong> Select in Containment Tree</strong>.</li></ul><p> </p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2fba24b3-7c73-4698-a562-231e40bd460e"><ac:parameter ac:name="">decompose</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To decompose an element</p><hr /><ol><li>Select an element.</li><li>Do one of the following:<ul><li>From the selected element shortcut menu, select <strong>Create Related Element</strong>.</li><li>Click <ac:image ac:title="Create Related Element" ac:alt="Create Related Element"><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_element_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> or press Insert (or <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:width="16"><ri:attachment ri:filename="command-button.jpg"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>+ I).</li></ul></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Select element type and relationship.  If selected element type exists in the model, select an appropriate element from the list to represent it in the relation map. <ac:link ac:anchor="open"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[How to open an existing element list for the unnamed last element >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><br />The element is decomposed.</p></li></ol><p><br /></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="20193be0-2b01-422f-9587-a0ecd1639aab"><ac:parameter ac:name="">open</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To open an existing element list for the unnamed element</p><hr /><ol><li>Select unnamed element.</li><li>Do one of the following:<br /> - Click the left mouse button.<br /> - Press Space.<br />An existing element list opens, if such elements already exist in the model.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f6481eb5-ed02-4c78-a43f-0bef60502651"><ac:parameter ac:name="">same_type</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To create element of the same type</p><hr /><ol><li>Type element name.</li><li>Press Ctrl+Enter while in the name edit mode.</li></ol><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243968437 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 05624: Manual numbering

- page_id: `243968437`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243968437/Manual+numbering
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:59.092+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Working with model elements > Generic numbering mechanism > Working with generic numbering mechanism
- labels: ['how-to-update-the-last-id', 'msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

INLINE

Using the manual numbering, you can:

- Set the ID for one or more elements.
- Increase, decrease, or change a number for an element.
- Add a prefix for the element number.
- Change a numbering format.
- Update the last ID.

To number the element

1. On the selected element’s shortcut menu, click Element Numbering . The Element Numbering dialog opens.
2. In the element list on the right side of the dialog, select an element you want to number.
3. Click Create .

To change the element’s numbering symbol

1. Open the selected element’s shortcut menu and click Element Numbering . The Element Numbering dialog opens.
2. Click the Edit button.
  - If you need to change just the last number of the element ID, click **Edit**. The **Edit ID** dialog opens. 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] You can enter only the number, if the selected element’s numbering value is numerical. 
You can enter only a new literal symbol, if the selected element’s numbering value is a literal symbol.
  - If you need to change the whole element ID (including the prefix), click Set Custom ID . The Custom ID dialog opens. [ATTACHMENT filename='Custom_ID_dialog.png']
3. Enter a new numbering symbol.
4. Click **OK**.

To remove the element’s number

1. Open the selected element’s shortcut menu and click Element Numbering . The Element Numbering dialog opens.
2. Select the element from which you want to remove the number and click the Remove button.
3. You can select either to remove the number only from the selected element or to remove numbers from all elements owned by the selected elements. Do one of the following:
  - Click the Remove button. Only the selected element’s number is removed.
  - Click the Remove Recursively button. Numbers are removed from the selected element and all elements owned by the selected element.
4. Click OK when you are done.

To increase the element number

1. Open the selected element’s shortcut menu and click Element Numbering . The Element Numbering dialog opens.
2. Select the element which number you want to increase.
3. Click the Increase button. The element number is increased by one.
4. Click OK when you are done.

To decrease the element number

1. Open the selected element’s shortcut menu and select Element Numbering . The Element Numbering dialog opens.
2. Select the element which number you want to decrease.
3. Click the **Decrease** button. The element number is decreased by one. The **Decrease** button is available, when the selected element’s number is not the first in the list.
4. Click OK when you are done.

To renumber the elements

1. On the selected element’s shortcut menu, click Element Numbering . The Element Numbering dialog opens.
2. Click Details to extent the dialog with the renumbering commands.
3. You can select either to renumber only elements listed in the element list or to renumber elements listed in the element list including their owning elements. Do one of the following:
  - Click the **Renumber** button. All the elements in the elements list are renumbered successively.
  - Click the **Renumber Recursively** button. All the elements and elements owned by the selected element are renumbered successively.
4. Click **OK** when you are done.

If the **Renumber from Initial Value** is selected, the whole element number changes. Otherwise just the number of the selected level changes.

To add a prefix to the element number

1. On the element owner’s shortcut menu, select Element Numbering . The Element Numbering dialog opens.
2. In the Prefix column, click the appropriate cell (according to the used numbering property and numbering scheme).
3. Type the prefix. It can be any symbol.
4. Click OK .

The following example illustrates how numbers with prefixes and without prefixes are depicted on the element shape.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To change a separator in the element number

1. Open the element owner’s shortcut menu and click Element Numbering . The Element Numbering dialog opens.
2. In the Separator column, click the appropriate cell (according to the used numbering property and numbering scheme).
3. Type the separator symbol. A new separator will override the earlier predefined separator.
4. Click OK .

The following example illustrates the number separator changing from “.” to “/”.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To change a numbering scheme

1. On the selected element’s shortcut menu, click Element Numbering . The Element Numbering dialog opens.
2. In the appropriate Numbered property row, click the Numbering Scheme cell to open the list of the defined numbering schemes.
3. Select the desired numbering scheme in that list.
4. Click OK .

When you change the numbering scheme for the selected element, the new numbering scheme is applied to all elements owned by the selected element.

The following example illustrates how numbers are depicted on the element shape after changing the numbering scheme from numerical to literal.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To update the last ID

1. Right-click an element numbered in the sequence, whose last ID you want to update.
2. From the shortcut menu, select Element Numbering . The appropriate dialog opens.
3. Click Details and then click the Update Last ID button.
4. Wait while the message informs you that the last ID is set to the particular value. This value is the highest ID in the sequence.
5. Click OK to close the Element Numbering dialog.

As a result, all subsequently created elements are numbered continuing from the the highest ID. This is especially useful after you delete one or more elements from the end of the numbered sequence. In such cases, the last ID remains higher than it is expected to be, causing a numbering gap in the sequence. Updating the last ID prevents the emergence of that gap.

However, if a numbering gap is not at the end of the sequence, it remains even after the update of the last ID.

For better understanding the functionality, let's study a few cases.

1. If you have a sequence from 1 to 5, and delete elements with ID = 4 and ID = 5, the ID of a subsequently created element by default is 6. But if you update the last ID in advance, the one of the subsequently created element is 4 as it should be.
2. If you have a sequence from 1 to 3 and from 5 to 6, the last ID after the update is 6. As you can see, ID = 4 is skipped, because it isn't the highest ID in the sequence; the highest ID is 6.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

## PAGE 05625: Manual Task

- page_id: `249566502`
- space_key: `MT`

- version_date: `2025-08-18T18:22:05.781+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > BPMN modeling > Business Process Model and Notation Concepts > BPMN Process Diagram > Activities > Tasks
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1486572542

1486572542

1486572542

**Description**

A Manual Task is a task that is expected to be performed without the aid of any business process execution engine or application, for example, installing a telephone at a customer location.

**Notation**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

1486572541

**Related elements**

- Activities
- Task
- Service Task
- Send Task
- Receive Task
- User Task
- Business Rule Task
- Script Task

**Related diagrams**

- BPMN Process Diagram
- BPMN Collaboration Diagram

**Related procedure**

- Creating and Using Tasks

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

Create a HashMap instance.

## PAGE 05627: Mapping issues

- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249565187/Mapping+issues
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-09-15T08:53:40.244+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > Procedures > Importing from ArchiMate
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### Instance Specifications

When the elements imported from ArchiMate are mapped to the UAF Instance Specification, the predefined types from ArchiMate Customization are assigned during the import. The following table shows how the predefined types are assigned:

| # | ArchiMate 3.1 Element | UAF 1.3 |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Element | Type/Classifier | Predefined Type |  |  |
| 1. | Location | Actual Location | Location |  |
| 2. | Outcome | Actual Measurement Set | Measurement Set |  |
| 3. | Plateau | Actual Enterprise Phase | Strategic Phase |  |
| 4. | Stakeholder | Actual Organization | Organization |  |
| 5. | Deliverable | Actual Project Milestone | Project Milestone |  |
| 6. | Implementation Event | Actual Project Milestone | Project Milestone |  |
| 7. | Work Package | Actual Project | Project |  |

##### Relation import exceptions

There are several exceptions when importing relations from ArchiMate. The following are cases when the ArchiMate relation after the import is not directly mapped to UAF relationships, and the property is created instead of the relationship:

- Events:
  - If the ArchiMate Application Event or Technology Event is related to an ArchiMate Element using an Association, and the ArchiMate Element maps to a UAF Resource Performer, after the import, no relationship is created. Instead, the reception to the Resource Signal is created for the Resource Performer.
  - If ArchiMate Business Event is related to ArchiMate Element using Association, and the ArchiMate Element maps to UAF Operational Performer, after the import, no relationship is created. Instead, the reception to the Operational Signal is created for the Operational Performer.
- Constraints:
  - If the ArchiMate Element is related to the Constraint element using any relation, after the import, no relationship is created. Instead, the Constraint is applied to the UAF Element.
  - If the ArchiMate Constraint has any relationship (a) with another relationship (b) (except for the relationship that connects the Constraint with any other ArchiMate relationship), then the Constraint will be applied to the imported UAF relationship (b) during the import process, and no relationship (a) will be created. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
- Annotated elements:
  - Any ArchiMate element related to the Grouping element using any relation, after the import is added to the UAF Element grouping [Comment] as the Annotated element. No relationship is created.
  - Any ArchiMate element related to the Notes element using Connection/Line, after the import, is added to the UAF Information [Comment] element as the Annotated element. No relationship is created.

##### Complex import and mapping depending on related ArchiMate elements and views

ArchiMate Driver and Assessment import cases:

- If ArchiMate Driver is directly related to any other PropertySet (e.g., Capability) using Association, then after the import the PropertySet with Driver as a measurement is created. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
- If the ArchiMate Assessment has no Association relation to other elements, after the import, it maps to MeasurementSet + Measurement + Default Value. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
- If the ArchiMate Assessment name contains a value (e.g., Range=180nm) and has an Association relation with any other PropertySet (e.g., "Capability", ), but has no Association relation to Driver, then after the import Assessment is created as Measurement for PropertySet and ActualPropertySet is created with slots displaying Assessment Name and value. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
- If ArchiMate Assessment has an Association relation with Driver and PropertySet (sequence Driver > Association > Assessment > Association > PropertySet), then after the import the Assessment is created as a Measurement for the PropertySet andActualMeasurementSets are created with slots displaying values from Assessments (slots are not created if there are no values in Assessments). Additionally, the ActualPropertySet is created for the PropertySet.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Services and Interfaces:

- If ArchiMate Business Interface is related to Business Service using the Assignment relation, then after the import the UAF Service with Service Port which type is Service Interface is created.
- If ArchiMate Technology/Application Interface is related to Technology/Application Service using the Assignment relation, then after the import the UAF Resource Artifact with Resource Port which type is Resource Interface is created. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The same element is used in several ArchiMate views:

- If the same ArchiMate element is used in several ArchiMate views and can be mapped to several UAF elements according to mapping rules matching in different views (e.g., depending on relations, Archimate Plateau can be mapped either to Capability Configuration or Actual Enterprise Phase), then the element is imported and mapped to only one UAF element according to one rule.
- If certain elements, such as ArchiMate Business Processes/Functions, Application Processes/Functions, Technology Processes/Functions, Business/Application/Technology Events, or And/Or junctions, are used in multiple ArchiMate Views and are converted to activity/function + action under different domain owners (Operational/Resource/Service) and are connected using Flow or Triggering relationships, then these elements will be imported and mapped correctly according to the rules under different domain owners. However, the Flow and Triggering relationships that map to the Operational Function Control Flow or Service Function Control Flow will only be mapped according to one rule and will be owned by only one random domain owner.

##### Failed Elements and Relationships

Elements and relationships are listed as failed elements during the import from the ArchiMate model in the following cases:

- If the junctions (And, Or) are:
  - without relations
  - related to the Business/Technology/Application Process, Business/Technology/Application Function, Business/Technology/Application Event using other than Triggering relation.
  - not related to Business/Technology/Application Process, Business/Technology/Application Function, Business/Technology/Application Event elements using the Triggering relation.
- If it is any other relation than Triggering that relates to/from junctions (And/Or).
- If it is a relationship from/to failed Junction (And/Or).
- Duplicated relations.
- A relationship to itself (when source and target are the same elements):
  - The ArchiMate Specialization relationship, which after the import maps to Generalization or Containment.
  - The ArchiMate Composition relationship, which after the import maps to Containment.
- Operational Exchange between Operational Activity Actions or Resource Exchange between Function Actions if actions are created from ArchiMate Business, Application, Technology Process, or Function related to Assignment relation to Business, Application, Technology Interface.
- A relationship which connects Constraint and any other ArchiMate relationship which connects Constraint and any other ArchiMate element. [ATTACHMENT filename='constraint_any.png']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243966465 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 05629: Mapping ports on a Part type change

- page_id: `243966465`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243966465/Mapping+ports+on+a+Part+type+change
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:03.004+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Diagrams > UML diagrams > Composite Structure diagram
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

On a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Part' space='MED'] type change, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Port' space='MED'] of the original Part type are automatically replaced with ports of a new type. Connectors are reconnected accordingly too.

Ports are mapped automatically if particular rules are met. Otherwise, the **Port Mapping** dialog appears.

##### Rules of automatic port mapping

Ports are mapped automatically:

- When the following properties of the Port of the original Part type are the same as the properties of the Port of the new part type:
  - Port type and name
  - Port type (note that only one Port of the new Part type has the same type as Port(s) of the original Part type)
  - Port name (valid if the type of the original Port is not specified)
- Ports of the original Part type and the Port of the new Part type has the same [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Interface' space='MED'] .
- When the new Part type is subtype of the original Part type (inherited ports are mapped automatically with ports which they are inherited).
- When the ports of a new type redefines ports of original Part type.

##### The Port Mapping dialog

The **Port Mapping** dialog appears on changing a Part type when ports of the type cannot be mapped automatically.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

See the elements of the **Port Mapping** dialog described in the following table.

| Element number | Element | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 1 |  | Lists the type of the Part from which you want to make a replacement. Ports of the type are listed under the type. |
| 2 |  | Lists the type of the Part to which you want to make a replacement. Ports of the type are listed under the type. |
| 3 |  | Displays lines between the mapped ports. |
| 4 |  | Creates a new port in the To list. In the From list, click a port and then drag it to the "Drag port(s) here to create a new port" box. The port for the new type will be created and mapped. |

To map ports in the **Port Mapping** dialog

1. Select a Port in the From box.
2. Drag the selected Port onto a Port in the **To** box. The ports are mapped and the line between them is displayed. You can also select multiple number of ports in the **From** box and drag onto a port in the **To** box. The selected ports will be mapped with the Port.

To create a new Port in the **To** box

1. Select a Port in the From box.
2. Drag the selected Port onto the following box: "*Drag**port(s)**here**to**create**a**new**port*". The new Port is created in the **To** box and ports are mapped. You can also select multiple number of ports in the **From** box and drag onto the box: "*Drag port(s) here to create a new port*". The selected number of ports will be created.

To un-map ports in the **Port Mapping** dialog

1. Select the line between the mapped ports.
2. Press the Delete key. The line is deleted and ports unmapped.

The line is deleted and ports unmapped. </li></ol><p> </p><p style="text-align: center;" /></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249565605 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 05630: Mapping Problems and Solutions

- page_id: `249565605`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249565605/Mapping+Problems+and+Solutions
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T16:31:38.535+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > Migration User Guides > UPDM projects migration manual > UPDM 1.x Migration to UPDM 2.0
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### Using To Do Property

Going through the above sections of the manual, you have probably noticed that a large number of element properties were marked “added to the toDo property”. It indicates that data which cannot be directly mapped or has been removed during migration will be placed in the element's To Do property.

For instance, if you had Supports Operational Activity before the migration, the information about it will be stored in the ToDo property of Service Interface (source of the relationship).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Additionally to the fact that the Service Interface supported Operational Activity, Supports Operational Activity relationship name and other UPDM properties that had values assigned will be added to the toDo property.

Use the **Find ToDo** command to search for elements containing **ToDo** information. From the **Edit** main menu, choose **Find ToDo**.

##### Displaying Related Elements

Newly created relationships are not displayed in the diagrams by default. The reason for this is to prevent the damage on the diagrams. However, you can easily visualize the information, existing in the model, on the diagram of your choice.

To easily display newly created relationships, select the element that is the source or the target of the relationship and from the shortcut menu select **Related Elements** > **Display Related Elements**.

For instance, relationship between Entity Item and Information Element has been realized through a representedBy property of the Entity Item. It is now changed to the relationship Details. However in diagram relationships are not displayed by default. In order to display the relationships, right-click one Entity Item at a time and from the shortcut menu select **Related Elements** > **Display Related Elements**.

Make sure the Dependency «Details» entry is checked in the**Display Related Elements** dialog and click **OK**.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Related elements and relationships appear on the diagram.

##### Preserving Needline and Resource Interface Data

According to UPDM 2.0, specification Needlines and Resource Interfaces can only be used in a particular context such as Logical or Physical Architecture or simply inside the another Performer/Node. In other words, these relationships can only be used to model internal structures.

To preserve the data of the Needlines and Resource Interfaces, Needlines are changed to Node Associations and Resource Interfaces to Resource Associations. From the first sight, differences on the diagram are barely visible.

Node Association and Resource Association both are not a part of UPDM standard.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><h3>Using To Do Property</h3><p>Going through the above sections of the manual, you have probably noticed that a large number of element properties were marked “added to the toDo property”. It indicates that data which cannot be directly mapped or has been removed during migration will be placed in the element's To Do property.</p><p>For instance, if you had Supports Operational Activity before the migration, the information about it will be stored in the ToDo property of Service Interface (source of the relationship).</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="service interface.jpg" /></ac:image></p><p>Additionally to the fact that the Service Interface supported Operational Activity, Supports Operational Activity relationship name and other UPDM properties that had values assigned will be added to the toDo property.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="156a7e02-3678-403a-97ac-94ad52382c26"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Use the <strong>Find ToDo</strong> command to search for elements containing <strong>ToDo</strong> information. From the <strong>Edit</strong> main menu, choose <strong>Find ToDo</strong>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Displaying Related Elements</h3><p>Newly created relationships are not displayed in the diagrams by default. The reason for this is to prevent the damage on the diagrams. However, you can easily visualize the information, existing in the model, on the diagram of your choice.</p><p>To easily display newly created relationships, select the element that is the source or the target of the relationship and from the shortcut menu select <strong>Related Elements</strong> &gt; <strong>Display Related Elements</strong>.</p><p>For instance, relationship between Entity Item and Information Element has been realized through a representedBy property of the Entity Item. It is now changed to the relationship Details. However in diagram relationships are not displayed by default. In order to display the relationships, right-click one Entity Item at a time and from the shortcut menu select <strong>Related Elements</strong> &gt; <strong>Display Related Elements</strong>.</p><p>Make sure the Dependency «Details» entry is checked in the<strong> Display Related Elements</strong> dialog and click <strong>OK</strong>.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="related elements.jpg" /></ac:image></p><p>Related elements and relationships appear on the diagram. </p><h3>Preserving Needline and Resource Interface Data</h3><p>According to UPDM 2.0, specification Needlines and Resource Interfaces can only be used in a particular context such as Logical or Physical Architecture or simply inside the another Performer/Node. In other words, these relationships can only be used to model internal structures.</p><p>To preserve the data of the Needlines and Resource Interfaces, Needlines are changed to Node Associations and Resource Interfaces to Resource Associations. From the first sight, differences on the diagram are barely visible.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="205855c3-1064-46a1-9b9f-e4f4deffe96d"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Node Association and Resource Association both are not a part of UPDM standard.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249565501 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 05631: Mapping solutions and issues

- page_id: `249565501`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249565501/Mapping+solutions+and+issues
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T16:31:35.517+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > Migration User Guides > UAF projects migration manual > Migration from UAF 1.0 to UAF 1.1
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### Removed tag added to ToDo property

Condition::conditionKind tag was removed, so after the migration it will be added into ToDo property in the Specification window of the Condition element.

##### Changed Property Types

44

###### Changes for Condition and Environment

In earlier versions, the Activity Performable Under Condition property (owned by Activity) had the Condition as its value. After the migration, the newly created Actual Condition with the Condition set as a type, replaces this property.

The same is valid for :

- Environmental Context (owned by Measurement) value Condition.
- Required Environment (owned by Location Holder) value. If the value was Environment, the Actual Environment is created . I f the value was Condition, the Actual Condition is created.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Changes for Doctrine

In earlier versions the Doctrine property (owned by Capability Configuration) had Constraint for its value. In UAF 1.1 Constraint is replaced by Standard Operational Activity. After the migration, Capability Configuration is set as constrainedElement to Constraint Except if Constraint is Operational Constraint or Service Policy.

##### The SecurityProperty renamed

In UAF 1.1, the Security Property is renamed to Information Role and the new Data Role is implemented. The Information Role is used by the Operational Assets and the Data Role is used by the Resource Assets.

- Operational Asset - An abstract element used to group the elements of Operational Agent and Information Element.
- Resource Asset - An abstract element used to group the elements of Resource Performer and Data Element.

If Operational Agent and Information Element (in UAF 1.1 grouped by Operational Asset) had a Security Property set, after the migration the Security Property will be changed to:

- Information Role, if the type of the Security Property was any of the Operational Asset specializations.
- Simple UML property, if the type of the Security Property was Resource Asset.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

If Resource Performer and Data Element (in UAF 1.1 grouped by Resource Asset) had a Security Property set, after the migration the Security Property will be changed to:

- Data Role, if the type of the Security Property was Resource Asset.
- Simple UML property, if the type of the Security Property was Operational Asset.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### NOTE: Known Resource exception

Known Resource exception

The Known Resource is both: Resource Asset and Operational Asset. After the migration, the Security Property (owned by the Know Resource) is changed depending on its type:

- If the type was Data Element, the Security Property becomes Data Role.
- If the type was Information Element, the Security Property becomes Information Role.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

After the migration, the newly created Actual Condition with the Condition set as a type, replaces this propert<ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="7a243fc5-0832-49c4-9bb5-f64004a2f9e1">y.</ac:inline-comment-marker></p><p>The same is valid for :</p><ul><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="e3a0d88f-532d-4e71-9035-36fc184b61f8">Environmental Context (owned by Measurement) value Condition.</ac:inline-comment-marker></li><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="b75db304-ea1e-44e3-89a2-42ec9b4bfa48">Required</ac:inline-comment-marker> Environment (owned by Location Holder) value.  If the value was <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="6373d10f-6adf-4853-886e-c879c59757c4">Environment, the Actual Environment is created</ac:inline-comment-marker>. I<span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">f the value was Condition, the Actual Condition is created.</span></li></ul><p><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="concition_to_instance.png" /></ac:image></p><h4>Changes for Doctrine</h4><p>In earlier versions the Doctrine property (owned by Capability Configuration) had Constraint for its value. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243968526 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 05632: Marking elements as auxiliary

- page_id: `243968526`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243968526/Marking+elements+as+auxiliary
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:51:01.580+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Working with model elements
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

639285959

639285961

639285960

An auxiliary element is an element needed in the model but not necessarily visible in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model Browser' space='']. Usually some profiles are marked as auxiliary and are not displayed by default in the model. You can mark the element in your model as an auxiliary as well.

To mark an element as auxiliary

1. Select the element in the Model Browser.
2. In the Properties tab, select the Tags tab.
3. Set the treatAsAuxiliaryInOwningProject tag value to true .

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Setting the tag value.

The element marked as auxiliary is visible in the Model Browser if the option **Show Auxiliary Resources**is selected in the Options.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Options drop-down list.

You can specify that all auxiliary resources are shown in the model by default by specifying in the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customizing environment options' space='']**of your tool.

To show auxiliary resources by default in your model

1. On the main menu, click Options > Environment .
2. In the search box, type auxiliary . All auxiliary resources related properties appears in the options list.
3. Set the Show Auxiliary Resources property value to true .

639285958

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model Browser' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customizing environment options' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2860a193-7aa5-4455-b579-0308f79f4a03"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">639285959</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3f72600d-3170-4dc9-9573-e6d04928319c"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">639285961</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fc4aa923-7200-49b6-9232-8b3b619016db"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">639285960</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>An auxiliary element is an element needed in the model but not necessarily visible in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Model Browser" /></ac:link>. Usually some profiles are marked as auxiliary and are not displayed by default in the model. You can mark the element in your model as an auxiliary as well.</p><p><br /></p><p>To mark an element as auxiliary</p><hr /><ol><li>Select the element in the Model Browser.</li><li>In the <strong>Properties </strong>tab, select the <strong>Tags</strong> tab.</li><li>Set the <strong>treatAsAuxiliaryInOwningProject</strong> tag  value to <em>true</em>.</li></ol><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="tag_auxiliary.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="margin-left: 30.0px;text-align: center;">Setting the tag value.</h6><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><br /></p><p style="text-align: left;">The element marked as auxiliary is visible in the Model Browser if the option <strong>Show Auxiliary Resources </strong>is selected in the Options.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="show_auxiliary_resources.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="margin-left: 30.0px;text-align: center;">The Options drop-down list.</h6><p>You can specify that all auxiliary resources are shown in the model by default by specifying in the <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Customizing environment options" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Environment Options]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> </strong>of your tool.</p><p><br /></p><p>To show auxiliary resources by default in your model</p><hr /><ol><li>On the main menu, click <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Environment</strong>. </li><li>In the search box, type <em>auxiliary</em>. All auxiliary resources related properties appears in the options list.</li><li>Set the <strong>Show Auxiliary Resources </strong>property value to <em>true</em>.</li></ol><p><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b8ad7878-8e57-4933-8a55-70b379706605"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">639285958</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Model Browser" /></ac:link><strong><br class="_mce_tagged_br" /></strong></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Customizing environment options" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

```text

```

```text
fixed = boolean : false
```

```text
id = ID
```

```text
value = anySimpleType
```

```text
{any attributes with non-schema namespace…}>	Content: (annotation?)
```

```text

```

```text
{value} must be in the value space of {base type definition}.
```

```text
The following is the definition of a user-derived datatype which limits values to integers less than or equal to 100, using maxExclusive.
```

```

```text

```

```text

```

```text
</restriction>
```

```text
</simpleType›
```

```text
Note that the value space of this datatype is identical to the previous one (named 'one­-hundred-or-less').
```

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249566863 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 05636: Mean Concept Relationships

- page_id: `249566863`
- space_key: `MT`

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1487501502

1487501504

1487501504

The following table provides the relationships that exist between element types and their meaning.

| Relationship | Description | Example |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Makes Operative | This link connects to a . Meaning that the Mission lists every continuingActivity to ensure the Vision. |  |
| Component Of | This link connects to a . Meaning that the Strategy is the method orcourse of action that will be employed to achieve the Mission. |  |
| Implements | This link connects to a . Meaning that the Tactic realizes Strategies. |  |
| Enables | This link connects to a or to a . Meaning that a Strategy/Tactic makes another Strategy/Tactic doable. That is to say the latter Strategy/Tacticprovides an opportunity for the former Strategy/Tactic to be carried out. Use this linkto associate Strategy to another Strategy or Tactic to another Tactic. |  |
| Effects Enforcement Level | This link connects to a . Meaning that the Tactics influences the |  |
| Formulated Based On | This link connects or to a or . Meaningthat the Strategy or Tactic is planned according to what result the Business Policy orBusiness Rule desires. |  |
| Channel Efforts Towards | This link connects to a , or to an . Meaning that the Strategy iscoordinated as activities aimed towards Goals as Tactic towards Objective. For example, theTactic “Free delivery” channels efforts towards the Objective “5% increase in sales within 3months.” |  |
| Governs | This link connects or to a or . Meaning that the Business Policy or BusinessRule determines the Strategy or Tactic. |  |
| Basis For | This link connects to a . Meaning that the Business Policy is the key to achieve Business Rule. |  |
| Supports Achievement Of | This link connects or to a or . Meaning that the Business Policy provides anidea or reason to develop the Strategy or Tactic. |  |
| Acts As Regulation | This link connects a or a to External Influencer. |  |

1487501501

**Related element**

- Means Concepts

**Related diagrams**

- Business Motivation Diagram
- Process Definition Diagram

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249565281 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 05638: Measurements

- page_id: `249565281`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249565281/Measurements
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T16:25:36.777+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > Procedures
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A measurement is a property of an element representing something in the physical world, expressed in amounts of a unit of measure. You can create measurements either in the measurements table or on the element shape in the diagram .

Note, that when you display the measurements in the measurements compartment, you can choose to show the inherited names through the dot notation when the Property Set is applied as a type. In this case, the original name of the measurements will no longer be shown. However, if you need to see the original name, set the **Show Original Name of Measurement** symbol property to true. The original name will be displayed in brackets and in gray font (see the image bellow).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Learn more about working with measurements:

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>A measurement is a property of an element representing something in the physical world, expressed in amounts of a unit of measure. You can create measurements either in the measurements table or on the element shape in the diagram .</p><p>Note, that when you display the measurements in the measurements compartment, you can choose to show the inherited names through the dot notation when the Property Set is applied as a type. In this case, the original name of the measurements will no longer be shown. However, if you need to see the original name, set the <strong>Show Original Name of Measurement</strong> symbol property to true. The original name will be displayed in brackets and in gray font (see the image bellow).</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="dot_notation_measurements.png" /></ac:image></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="original_measurement_name.png" /></ac:image></p><p><br /></p><p>Learn more about working with measurements:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="2c2b0623-cd0e-4255-b0a4-ebeb8a94756b" /></p><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249564918 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 05639: Measurements dialog

- page_id: `249564918`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249564918/Measurements+dialog
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:07:26.141+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > Dialogs
- labels: ['measurements-dialog', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1004957137

1004957139

1004957138

The **Measurements** dialog is designed to display measurements available to be added as columns to the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-3 Operational Resource Flow Matrix' space=''] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-6 Systems Resource Flow Matrix' space=''] tables.

To open the **Measurements** dialog

- On the table toolbar, click Show Columns button and select Measurements . The Measurements dialog opens.

The **Measurements** dialog consists of two tabs:

- Operational Exchange (or **Resource****Exchange** in the SV-6 table).
- Operational Exchange Item (or Resource ExchangeItem in the SV-6 table).

The**Operational Exchange** (or **Resource****Exchange**in the SV-6 and table) tab displays measurements grouped by the Measurement Sets of the Operational Exchanges (or Resource Exchanges in the SV-6 and SvcV-6 tables). The **Operational Exchange Item** (or**Resource ExchangeItem** in the SV-6 table) tab displays measurements grouped by the Measurement Sets of Operational Exchange Items (or Resource Exchange Items in the SV-6 table). The measurements displayed in the **Measurements** dialog are collected from the active table.

The purpose of the Measurement Set is grouping the particular measurements. In order to show or hide measurements of a particular Measurement Set, you can expand or collapse the Measurement Set category. For example, Exchange Properties is the grouping Measurement Set of the measurements Accountability, Interoperability Level Achievable, and so forth.

When you select the measurement and click the **OK** button, the selected measurement is added as a new column into the table.

The selected measurement description is displayed in the Description area. If you want to see the description of your custom measurement, you should have specified the documentation when creating a measurement.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

1004957135

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying Custom Measurements' space='']

The <strong>Operational Exchange Item</strong> (or<strong> Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange </span>Item</strong> in the SV-6 table) tab displays measurements grouped by the Measurement Sets of Operational Exchange Items (or Resource Exchange Items in the SV-6 table). The measurements displayed in the <strong>Measurements</strong> dialog are collected from the active table.</p><p>The purpose of the Measurement Set is grouping the particular measurements. In order to show or hide measurements of a particular Measurement Set, you can expand or collapse the Measurement Set category. For example, Exchange Properties is the grouping Measurement Set of the measurements Accountability, Interoperability Level Achievable, and so forth.</p><p>When you select the measurement and click the <strong>OK</strong> button, the selected measurement is added as a new column into the table.</p><p>The selected measurement description is displayed in the Description area. If you want to see the description of your custom measurement, you should have specified the documentation when creating a measurement.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="Measurements dialog" ac:alt="Measurements dialog"><ri:attachment ri:filename="measurements_dialog.png" /></ac:image></p><p><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5c20c3f4-ad7d-4b7b-932c-b5b0680b5711"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1004957135</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Displaying Custom Measurements" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249565580 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 05641: Measurements migration

- page_id: `249565580`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249565580/Measurements+migration
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T16:31:37.893+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > Migration User Guides > UPDM projects migration manual > UPDM 2.1 migration to UAF 1.1 > Mapping issues and solutions
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

If the UAF element is a Property Set (e.g. Operational Performer, Resource Performer, Information Element, Service Specification, Capability), the new measurement modeling approach is applied after the migration from UPDM 2.

The new approach includes:

- If an element has Property Set property with value (Measure Type or Measurement Set), then the new Measurement attribute is created under the element with the type Measure Type / Measurement Set.
- If an element has an Actual Property Set with value Actual Measurement Set , then the instance of that element is created. The instance gets a slot which defining feature is a Measurement attribute from the element. The value of that slot is the element which was set in the Actual Property Set property.
- Measurement attribute is created for each value in the Property Set property.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example for the measurements migration from UPDM 2 to UAF.

If an element is a measurable element (e.g. Operational Activity, Operational Exchange, Resource Role), the approach of applying measurements stays the same as in UPDM 2. Property Set property is renamed to Measurement Set. Actual Property Set property is renamed to Actual Property Set.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>If the UAF element is a Property Set (e.g. Operational Performer, Resource Performer, Information Element, Service Specification, Capability), the new measurement modeling approach is applied after the migration from UPDM 2.</p><p>The new approach includes:</p><ul><li>If an element has Property Set property with value (Measure Type or Measurement Set), then the new Measurement attribute is created under the element with the type Measure Type / Measurement Set.</li><li>If an element has an Actual Property Set with value Actual Measurement Set , then the instance of that element is created. The instance gets a slot which defining feature is a Measurement attribute from the element. The value of that slot is the element which was set in the Actual Property Set property.</li><li>Measurement attribute is created for each value in the Property Set property.</li></ul><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="measurements_migration.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">An example for the measurements migration from UPDM 2 to UAF.</h6><p>If an element is a measurable element (e.g. Operational Activity, Operational Exchange, Resource Role), the approach of applying measurements stays the same as in UPDM 2. Property Set property is renamed to Measurement Set. Actual Property Set property is renamed to Actual Property Set.</p>
````

The new approach includes:

- If an element has Property Set property with value (Measure Type or Measurement Set), then the new Measurement attribute is created under the element with the type Measure Type / Measurement Set.
- If an element has an Actual Property Set with value Actual Measurement Set , then the instance of that element is created. The instance gets a slot which defining feature is a Measurement attribute from the element. The value of that slot is the element which was set in the Actual Property Set property.
- Measurement attribute is created for each value in the Property Set property.

If an element is a measurable element (e.g. Operational Activity, Operational Exchange, Resource Role), the approach of applying measurements stays the same as in UPDM 2. Property Set property is renamed to Measurement Set. Actual Property Set property is renamed to Actual Property Set.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243965844 space=MT version=3 -->
## PAGE 05643: Memory allocation

- page_id: `243965844`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243965844/Memory+allocation
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2026-06-25T13:46:55.484+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > Getting started > Improving performance
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Low memory notification

1427463063

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Low memory notification

1427463065

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Low memory notification

1427463064

If you receive an error message saying your modeling tool is out of memory, the application does not have enough memory to operate. You can change the amount of allocated memory directly in the error message dialog or by changing modeling tool properties.

#### TIP: Memory allocation concept

Memory allocation concept

Memory allocation for the product is the same as the Java heap size. To increase the amount of memory allocated for the product, simply increase the Java heap size.

##### Recommended amount of allocated memory

The amount of allocated memory (or Java heap size) should be set to less than the available physical RAM on your computer. To calculate the approximate amount of memory you should allocate, subtract the amount of memory taken by any other processes that will run concurrently with your modeling tool from the total amount of RAM in your computer. Otherwise, your JVM process will likely swap, and that will slow down the application.

##### Changing the amount of allocated memory

You can set the amount of memory allocated for your modeling tool either in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customizing environment options' space='']**Environment Options** dialog, in the modeling tool properties file, or in the **Environment Variables** dialog on your machine.

To change the amount of allocated memory in the **Environment Options** dialog

1. In the main menu of your modeling tool, go to Options > Environment . The Environment Options dialog opens.
2. Expand the General options group on the left side of the dialog, and select the Memory Settings category.
3. In the option specification area on the right side of the dialog, change the value of the Maximum Heap Size option. You should only change the value of the Thread Stack Size option if a *java.lang.StackOverflowError*error occurs.

#### TIP: Low memory notification

Low memory notification

While working on the project, you can get the *Low memory* notification. This notification is triggered by the mechanism which tracks whether the allocated amount of memory is enough to work with your currently opened projects. Click *Open Environment Options*to open the **Environment options** dialog where you can adjust memory settings.

To enable/disable notifications about low memory

1. Go to Environment Options > Display > Notify about low memory option

- 
  - Set to true to show notifications about low memory.
  - Set to false to disable notifications about low memory. Also, t his notification can be disabled, by clicking Do not show again option in the Low memory notification.

To change the amount of allocated memory in the modeling tool properties file

1. Go to <modeling tool installation directory>\bin and open the modeling tool properties file.
2. In the **JAVA_ARGS** line, change the value of the **-Xmx** property. For example, change the **-Xmx800M** to **-Xmx1066M**.
3. Save and close the file.
4. Restart your modeling tool.

To change the amount of allocated memory in the **Environment Variables** dialog on your machine

1. Go to Control Panel > System and Security > System > Advanced system settings. The System Properties dialog opens.
2. In the Advanced tab, click Environment Variables . The Environment Variables dialog opens.
3. In the System variables section, do one of the following:
  1. If the _JAVA_OPTIONS variable is not in the variables list, click New . New System Variable dialog opens.
  2. If the **_JAVA_OPTIONS** variable is in the variables list, select it and click **Edit**.**Edit System Variable**dialog opens.
4. Set **Variable name** to **_JAVA_OPTIONS** and **Variable value** to the desired memory amount, e.g. **-Xmx1066M**. Click **OK**. Changing options via the **Environment Variables** dialog overrides specified options for all Java applications.

1427463062

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Improving performance' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Background Task Manager' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Memory monitor' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Active validation period' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243973890 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 05644: Memory and Disk Space Requirements for Large Models

- page_id: `243973890`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243973890/Memory+and+Disk+Space+Requirements+for+Large+Models
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-25T11:59:51.439+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Guidelines for working with large models
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### Memory

The memory allocation for the modeling tool by project size:

| Project Elements Count(including used projects) | Allocated Memory 2 |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Earlier versions | 2021x and later 1 |  |
| 1.5 million | 1 million | 6GB |
| 2.5 million | 2 million | 7GB |
| 5 million | 3.5 million | 11GB |

The amount of required memory proportionally increases if working on multiple projects.

1the project elements count is different due to the profiling data storing metamodel optimized in 2021x (decrease by 40-80% in SysML v1 and UAF projects).

2these memory requirements are indicative and may vary by project specifics.

The operations that require more memory (1, 2, and 3.5 million of 2021x project elements respectively):

- UPDM migration to UAF - 10GB, 10GB, 36GB
- Converting between UAF, DoDAF2, DoDAF, MODAF, NAF, NAF4 frameworks - 8GB, 9GB, 13GB
- Project Merge - 11GB, 13GB, 21GB 3

3strongly depends on the amount of detected changes between merged versions (in this case, the specified required memory is for merging projects with 1.5k changes in total).

##### Disk Space

The recommended disk space for installation and configuration files is 3GB or more depending on used plugins, project type (local or server), and project size. In addition to this, large server projects require extra disk space for configuration files:

| Project Elements Count(including used projects) | Disk Space |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 19.0 | 2021x |  |
| 1.5 million | 1 million | 6GB |
| 2.5 million | 2 million | 10GB |
| 5 million | 3.5 million | 30GB |

The amount of required disk space proportionally increases if working on multiple projects.

**Note:** Element History and Content History features usage requires significantly more disk space due to intensive caching at the client side.

Depending on the feature usage scenario (e.g., retrieving content history from old/distant project versions multiple times), the disk space might increase by up to 100GB.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
